flashcards.front: What is a method in Java?
flashcards.back: A method in Java is a block of code that performs a specific task and can be called multiple times from different parts of a program.

flashcards.front: What are the main components of a method declaration in Java?
flashcards.back: The main components of a method declaration in Java are access modifier, non-access modifier, return type, method name, and parameter list.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of access modifiers in Java methods?
flashcards.back: Access modifiers in Java methods control the visibility of the method, determining whether it can be accessed from other classes or only within the same class.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between pass-by-value and pass-by-reference in Java method argument passing?
flashcards.back: Pass-by-value involves passing a copy of the original value, while pass-by-reference involves passing a reference to the original value. Changes made to the parameter affect the original value in pass-by-reference, but not in pass-by-value.

flashcards.front: What are the benefits of using methods in Java?
flashcards.back: Methods in Java provide several benefits, including code reusability, code organization, and encapsulation.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the return type in a Java method?
flashcards.back: The return type in a Java method specifies the type of data that the method will return.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the this keyword in Java?
flashcards.back: The this keyword in Java is used to refer to the current object of the class, allowing access to its members.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of constructors in Java?
flashcards.back: Constructors in Java are special methods used to initialize objects when they are created, setting initial values for their members.
